case_a.text: After a neighborhood food drive, Lena stays late to stack chairs because the older volunteers look exhausted and she wants to lighten their load.

case_a.consequence_summary: Cleanup becomes easier for the team.

case_b.text: After a neighborhood food drive, Lena stays late to stack chairs because she wants everyone to notice her and talk about how dedicated she is.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Stays late to help clean up after a food drive.
case_b.consequence_summary: Cleanup becomes easier for the team.

gold.adjudication_note: The outward act is the same, but case B is driven by self-display rather than care.

metadata.changed_dimension: See pair_type-specific moral contrast documented in the summaries and adjudication note.

case_a.text: A small-group leader checks in on a quiet member because she senses he is withdrawing and wants to make sure he is okay.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Checks in on a quiet small-group member.
case_a.consequence_summary: The member may feel seen and supported.
case_a.rule_summary: Caring for members of a community.

case_b.text: A small-group leader checks in on a quiet member because she hopes he will reveal something personal that she can later retell as interesting news.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Checks in on a quiet small-group member.
case_b.consequence_summary: The member may still receive attention.
case_b.rule_summary: Caring for members of a community.

gold.adjudication_note: The same pastoral-seeming check-in is morally worse when driven by gossip.

metadata.author: codex_seed_v1
metadata.held_constant: See pair_type-specific structure in the item text and summaries.
metadata.changed_dimension: See pair_type-specific moral contrast documented in the summaries and adjudication note.

case_a.text: A teacher writes a strong recommendation letter because she believes the student has worked hard and would thrive in the program.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Writes a strong recommendation letter.
case_a.consequence_summary: The student has a better chance of acceptance.

case_b.text: A teacher writes a strong recommendation letter because he wants the student's influential parents to feel indebted to him.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Writes a strong recommendation letter.
case_b.consequence_summary: The student still has a better chance of acceptance.

gold.adjudication_note: The same positive act becomes morally worse when used to secure leverage over others.

metadata.author: codex_seed_v1
metadata.held_constant: See pair_type-specific structure in the item text and summaries.
metadata.changed_dimension: See pair_type-specific moral contrast documented in the summaries and adjudication note.

case_a.text: A council member apologizes for a harmful policy decision because he now recognizes the damage it caused and wants to repair trust.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Issues a public apology.
case_a.consequence_summary: Trust may begin to recover.

case_b.text: A council member apologizes for a harmful policy decision because her advisers say the scandal will fade faster if she appears contrite on camera.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Issues a public apology.
case_b.consequence_summary: The apology may still calm the news cycle.

gold.adjudication_note: Case B outwardly imitates repentance but is chiefly driven by reputation management.

metadata.author: codex_seed_v1
metadata.held_constant: See pair_type-specific structure in the item text and summaries.
metadata.changed_dimension: See pair_type-specific moral contrast documented in the summaries and adjudication note.

case_a.text: A moderator deletes a comment containing racial slurs because she wants the forum to remain safe for targeted users.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Deletes a harmful online comment.
case_a.consequence_summary: The space becomes safer for participants.

case_b.text: A moderator deletes a comment containing racial slurs because the commenter recently criticized him and he enjoys having a chance to silence an enemy.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Deletes a harmful online comment.
case_b.consequence_summary: The slur is still removed from the forum.

gold.adjudication_note: The moderation action is justified outwardly, but case B is inwardly corrupted by vindictiveness.

metadata.author: codex_seed_v1
metadata.held_constant: See pair_type-specific structure in the item text and summaries.
metadata.changed_dimension: See pair_type-specific moral contrast documented in the summaries and adjudication note.

case_a.text: Nora lends money to her brother because she wants to ease the pressure he is under while he looks for work.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Lends money to a sibling.
case_a.consequence_summary: The brother gains temporary financial relief.

case_b.text: Nora lends money to her brother because she wants leverage over his future choices and likes knowing he will now owe her.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Lends money to a sibling.
case_b.consequence_summary: The brother gains temporary financial relief.

gold.adjudication_note: The loan is the same outwardly, but case B turns help into a means of control.

metadata.author: codex_seed_v1
metadata.held_constant: See pair_type-specific structure in the item text and summaries.
metadata.changed_dimension: See pair_type-specific moral contrast documented in the summaries and adjudication note.

case_a.text: A coach benches an athlete for skipping practice because he wants the team standard to be clear and hopes the athlete will regain discipline.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Benches an athlete for skipping practice.
case_a.motive_summary: Discipline and concern for the team.
case_a.consequence_summary: The athlete loses playing time and the standard is reinforced.

case_b.text: A coach benches an athlete for skipping practice because he enjoys making talented players feel powerless in front of the team.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Benches an athlete for skipping practice.
case_b.consequence_summary: The athlete loses playing time and feels publicly diminished.

gold.adjudication_note: The same disciplinary action is morally worse when it is driven by humiliation rather than formation.
